# kolm.ai - private AI stack for captured traffic, reviewed data, and local artifacts

> One OpenAI-compatible gateway. One local event lake. One reviewed data path.
> One signed `.kolm` artifact when a workflow is ready to run locally.

kolm.ai helps teams and solo developers turn the model calls they already make
into private AI systems they can inspect, improve, and run on their own
hardware. The product starts as a drop-in gateway for OpenAI-compatible clients,
captures telemetry locally, redacts sensitive fields, promotes reviewed rows
into train and eval datasets, then compiles repeated workflows into signed
artifacts with receipts and verifier state.

## What kolm.ai is

- A local-first AI gateway for OpenAI-compatible traffic.
- A telemetry lake for prompts, responses, cost, latency, provider, model,
 tool traces, privacy decisions, and lineage.
- A review queue for approving, correcting, rejecting, and labeling rows before
 they become train or eval data.
- A training and distillation control plane for repeated model workflows.
- A `.kolm` artifact format for portable, signed, verified local AI units.
- A runtime target catalog for laptops, servers, edge devices, and phone-class
 model backbones such as Gemma-family targets.
- A verification surface for receipts, eval splits, signatures, and production
 readiness.

## What kolm.ai is not

- Not a replacement for every frontier-model call.
- Not a claim that all workloads should be trained immediately.
- Not a promise that raw sensitive data is safe to send upstream.
- Not a marketplace-first product before the capture and optimization loop
 proves value.
- Not a generic autonomous-agent platform. Agent and workflow traces are
 captured so they can later compile into rules, models, or human checkpoints.

## Primary product loop

1. Connect your app by changing the model base URL.
2. Capture model calls into a local event lake.
3. Detect and redact sensitive data according to policy.
4. Review rows before they enter train or eval datasets.
5. Find repeated workflows and high-token quick wins.
6. Train or distill the repeat when there is enough approved evidence.
7. Compile the result into a signed `.kolm` artifact.
8. Verify the artifact against held-out data and receipt checks.
9. Run the artifact locally where it makes economic and privacy sense.
10. Keep capturing drift and supersede the artifact when better evidence wins.

## Primary URLs

- https://kolm.ai/ - homepage
- https://kolm.ai/product - full product surface
- https://kolm.ai/capture - OpenAI-compatible gateway and capture loop
- https://kolm.ai/docs - documentation map
- https://kolm.ai/docs/api - HTTP API reference
- https://kolm.ai/docs/quickstart - docs quickstart
- https://kolm.ai/quickstart - product quickstart
- https://kolm.ai/training - training tiers and distillation path
- https://kolm.ai/runtimes - runtime targets
- https://kolm.ai/models - model and backbone catalog
- https://kolm.ai/pricing - pricing
- https://kolm.ai/security - security posture
- https://kolm.ai/trust - trust and verification
- https://kolm.ai/spec - `.kolm` format and receipt specification
- https://kolm.ai/openapi.json - OpenAPI manifest
- https://kolm.ai/sitemap.xml - sitemap

- OpenAI SDK: https://kolm.ai/integrations/openai-sdk
- Anthropic SDK: https://kolm.ai/integrations/anthropic-sdk
- OpenRouter: https://kolm.ai/integrations/openrouter
- LiteLLM: https://kolm.ai/integrations/litellm
- Vercel AI SDK: https://kolm.ai/integrations/vercel-ai-sdk
- LangChain: https://kolm.ai/integrations/langchain
- Docker Compose: https://kolm.ai/integrations/docker-compose
- Environment variables: https://kolm.ai/integrations/env-vars

## Developer surfaces

- CLI reference: https://kolm.ai/docs/cli
- SDK reference: https://kolm.ai/docs/sdk
- Node SDK source install: `npm i github:sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`
- Python SDK docs: https://kolm.ai/sdks
- MCP server docs: https://kolm.ai/docs/connectors
- VS Code local extension docs: https://kolm.ai/install/vscode

## Account surfaces

The post-auth account UI covers connectors, captured traffic, the event lake,
privacy events, repeated workflows, opportunities, labeling, datasets,
simulations, bakeoffs, builds, artifacts, devices, storage, and billing.

## Important concepts

- Gateway: the OpenAI-compatible front door for model calls.
- Lake: the local event store that records AI calls and lineage.
- Privacy membrane: detection, redaction, blocking, and override policy before
 rows move across trust boundaries.
- Reviewed dataset: approved rows only, separated into train and holdout.
- Opportunity: a repeated or expensive workflow that could be optimized.
- Bakeoff: comparison between frontier calls, local candidates, rules, and
 distilled artifacts.
- `.kolm`: the portable artifact carrying implementation, eval state, receipt
 chain, manifest, and runtime target metadata.
- K-score: a weighted artifact score. It is not a claim of unconditional
 correctness; it is evidence about performance on the declared eval surface.
- Production-ready gate: verifier state that checks eval independence,
 signatures, receipt lineage, and runtime metadata before an artifact is
 treated as shippable.

## Current install posture

The honest source install path is:

```bash
npm i github:sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack
```

Package-registry, Homebrew, Docker, mobile SDK, and marketplace claims should
only be treated as available when their corresponding docs and verification
tests state that they are published.

## Repository

- Public repository: https://github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack
- Private working repository may contain unreleased development.
- Code license and text license are documented in the repository and public
 license page.

- General: https://kolm.ai/contact
- Security disclosures: https://kolm.ai/.well-known/security.txt
- Press: press@kolm.ai
